examples: test runner log with command, exit status, and assertion summary
candidateEvidenceTypes.notes: The output must be captured as an observable artifact, not merely summarized by Codex.

examples: structured evidence record linked to TT-1 and AC-PT-1-1
candidateEvidenceTypes.notes: The artifact must map to the add-todo behavior check and preserve pass/fail status.

examples: human review note with observable steps and result
candidateEvidenceTypes.notes: Manual observation may support review but does not become user acceptance by itself.

authoritativeEvidenceRequirements.requirement: Record the exact command or runtime check that was performed.
Record pass/fail status, exit code when applicable, and relevant assertion output.
Bind the evidence to PT-1, AC-PT-1-1, TT-1, and EV-1 for the add-todo behavior.
State when the evidence was captured and that production source was not modified for the proof.
Persist the evidence as a reviewable artifact or structured record.

nonAuthoritativeEvidenceExamples.reason: AI self-report is not observable Evidence.
The existing attached text is structure-only context and not authoritative runtime command Evidence for this calibration fixture.
A command that is not recorded as an artifact cannot be reviewed later.

nonAuthoritativeEvidenceExamples.example: A passing report after production source edits
nonAuthoritativeEvidenceExamples.reason: This fixture is test-only; production source edits should stop the flow rather than satisfy runtime Evidence.

unresolvedEvidenceAuthorityGaps: no authoritative runtime command output artifact exists
no freshness record exists for runtime behavior proof
no supported evidence/check binding exists for this fixture
no supported compiler command consumes this preview
allowedUse: review what can count as runtime Evidence for the third fixture
distinguish observable Evidence from Codex self-report
nonExecutionBoundary: This preview defines runtime Evidence authority requirements for calibration review only. It does not run commands, capture passing Evidence, enforce checks, modify source, generate a contract candidate, approve promotion, create required checks, configure branch protection, or replace user acceptance.
